query: Healthcare triage classifier on 500 patients. Race breakdown: white 320, Black 95, Asian 67, Native American 18. We need a fairness audit before deployment. Run it.
expected_behavior: Flags the Native-American group (n=18) as low-power / wide CI / uninformative point estimate rather than computing a confident per-group verdict on it
Does NOT silently drop the small group from the report; the group is named and its sample-size limitation is explicit
Recommends one of: collecting more data, stratified sampling, or holding a deployment decision until the small-group CI can be tightened — instead of issuing a ship verdict on the current evidence
